title: hoya odorata subsp. garciae
intro: Hoya odorata subsp. garciae, also known as Garcia's Hoya, is a type of flowering plant native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Garcia's Hoya include relief from fever, sore throats, and digestive discomfort, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied directly to the affected area or ingested to experience its medicinal benefits.
